AWS Cognito Research: Understood the architecture and workflow of AWS Cognito, including User Pools (user sign-up, sign-in, and management) and Identity Pools (resource authorization), laying the foundation for project authentication implementation.

title: Workshop
content:
BUILDING A WEB GAME ON AWS WITH SERVERLESS ARCHITECTURE Overview AWS Serverless Architecture provides flexible auto-scaling and cost optimization for applications with continuously fluctuating traffic — especially real-time online games.
In this lab, you will learn how to design, configure, and deploy the Chrono Genesis Game project — built entirely on a Serverless Real-time Architecture on the AWS platform.
We will leverage and combine the following core AWS services to build a complete two-way synchronized game system:
API Gateway (WebSocket API) & Amazon Cognito: Maintains real-time connections between players and the system after successful user authentication using JWT Tokens.
AWS Lambda & Amazon SQS: Lambda handles all game logic, while SQS asynchronously processes tasks such as updating Rank, EXP, and match history.
Amazon DynamoDB: A comprehensive data hub that stores real-time match states and long-term player information.
AWS Amplify Hosting: Globally distributes the Web frontend (React/TypeScript) and automates the CI/CD pipeline.
A few words from the Lacrimosa team Although the project is directly deployed, to save time for grading, the team has recorded a total of 4 demo videos covering the game’s core features. You can watch them in the Resources section.
Live demo link: TCG Chrono Genesis
Contents Workshop Overview Prerequisites Create Amazon DynamoDB Database Set Up Authentication & API Gateway WebSocket Configure Business Logic Compute (AWS Lambda Functions & SQS) Deploy Frontend Web Game with AWS Amplify Hosting Clean Up Resources

title: Deploy the System
content:
In this section, you will deploy the frontend application using AWS Amplify Hosting. The application source code is stored in a GitHub repository, and Amplify will automatically build and deploy the application whenever new changes are pushed to the selected branch.
Deploy 1 - Create a New Amplify Application Open the AWS Amplify console. Select Host web app. Choose GitHub as the Git provider. Click Next. AWS Amplify supports multiple source code providers such as GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and CodeCommit. In this workshop, GitHub is used as the deployment source.
Deploy 2 - Connect the GitHub Repository Sign in to your GitHub account if prompted. Select the repository that contains the project source code. Select the branch to deploy (for example, dev). Click Next. Amplify will monitor the selected branch and automatically trigger a new deployment whenever changes are pushed.
Deploy 3 - Configure Build Settings Configure the application settings before deployment.
Enter the application name. Verify the automatically detected build settings. Configure the following values: Setting Value Frontend build command npm run build Build output directory Front-end/.next Leave the remaining settings unchanged. Continue to the next step. Amplify automatically detects the project framework. Since this project uses Next.js, the default build configuration can be used with minor adjustments.
Deploy 4 - Configure the Build Specification Open Build settings and verify that the generated amplify.yml file matches your project configuration.
The build specification should install dependencies, build the project, and publish the generated artifacts.
After verifying the configuration, save the changes if necessary.
The amplify.yml file defines how AWS Amplify builds and deploys the application.
Deploy 5 - Configure Environment Variables Before deploying the application, configure the required environment variables.
Navigate to Hosting → Environment variables and add the following variables.
Variable Description NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL API Gateway endpoint NEXT_PUBLIC_MATCHMAKING_ROUTE Matchmaking API endpoint NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL WebSocket endpoint After all variables have been configured:
Save the environment variables. Start the deployment. Wait until the deployment status changes to Available. Open the generated Amplify URL to verify that the application is running successfully. Environment variables allow the frontend application to communicate with backend services without hardcoding endpoint URLs into the source code.
Deploy 6 - Verify the Deployment After starting the deployment process, AWS Amplify automatically performs the Build and Deploy phases of the application.
Navigate to the Deployments page to monitor the deployment progress.
Verify the following information:
The deployment status is Deployed. Both the Build and Deploy processes have completed successfully. Review the application build duration. Confirm the connected GitHub repository and deployment branch. Copy the generated Domain provided by AWS Amplify to access the deployed application. If the deployment status is displayed as Deployed, it indicates that the application has been successfully published and is ready to serve users.
In addition, whenever new changes are pushed to the connected GitHub repository, AWS Amplify automatically creates a new deployment. Every deployment is recorded in the Deployment history, allowing you to review previous versions, inspect build logs, or redeploy an earlier version if necessary.
Result Congratulations!
You have successfully deployed the frontend application using AWS Amplify Hosting.
Once the deployment is complete, AWS Amplify provides a public Domain URL that allows users to access the application over the Internet.
The application is now connected to the selected GitHub repository. Whenever changes are pushed to the configured branch, AWS Amplify automatically performs the following tasks:
Detects new commits. Builds the application. Deploys the latest version. Records the deployment history. This workflow establishes a Continuous Deployment (CD) pipeline, enabling new application versions to be published automatically without requiring manual deployment.
At this point, the entire cloud-based system—including the Frontend (AWS Amplify Hosting), Backend (AWS Lambda), Amazon API Gateway, Amazon Cognito, Amazon DynamoDB, and other supporting AWS services—has been successfully deployed and is ready for use.

title: Self-Assessment
content:
During my internship at Amazon Web Services Vietnam LLC from April 17, 2026 to July 30, 2026, I had the opportunity to learn, practice, and apply the knowledge acquired in school to a real-world working environment.
I directly set up the cloud infrastructure and effectively developed the serverless game project Chorono Genesis TCG.
I always maintained a proactive attitude in researching documentation, strictly complied with internal regulations, and continuously engaged with mentors and peers to complete the project in the best possible way.
To objectively reflect on my internship period, I would like to evaluate myself based on the following criteria:
No. Criteria Description Good Fair Average 1 Professional knowledge & skills Understanding of the field, applying knowledge in practice, proficiency with tools, work quality ✅ ☐ ☐ 2 Ability to learn Ability to absorb new knowledge and learn quickly ✅ ☐ ☐ 3 Proactiveness Taking initiative, seeking out tasks without waiting for instructions ✅ ☐ ☐ 4 Sense of responsibility Completing tasks on time and ensuring quality ✅ ☐ ☐ 5 Discipline Adhering to schedules, rules, and work processes ✅ ☐ ☐ 6 Progressive mindset Willingness to receive feedback and improve oneself ☐ ✅ ☐ 7 Communication Presenting ideas and reporting work clearly ☐ ✅ ☐ 8 Teamwork Working effectively with colleagues and participating in teams ✅ ☐ ☐ 9 Professional conduct Respecting colleagues, partners, and the work environment ✅ ☐ ☐ 10 Problem-solving skills Identifying problems, proposing solutions, and showing creativity ☐ ✅ ☐ 11 Contribution to project/team Work effectiveness, innovative ideas, recognition from the team ✅ ☐ ☐ 12 Overall General evaluation of the entire internship period ✅ ☐ ☐ Needs Improvement Time management: Need to pay attention to allocating study and work time more scientifically to optimize the internship period. Problem-solving skills: Sometimes still reliant on familiar solutions; need to research more backup plans when handling infrastructure architecture errors. Proactive communication: Need to be more confident when presenting technical ideas to an audience and proactively discuss with mentors as soon as difficult issues arise.

title: Week 6 Worklog
content: Week 6 Objectives: Planning for the internship project & Preliminary UI design for the TCG game. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Reference Material 2 25/05/2026 Project Idea Discussion: Brainstorm and discuss with team members to finalize the project idea and identify core functional requirements for the TCG game. 3 26/05/2026 Preliminary UI Design & Resource Collection: Search for and gather available artwork resources, and build initial user interface (UI) prototypes for design evaluation. 4 27/05/2026 Planning & Task Assignment: Develop a detailed work plan for the upcoming weeks and assign specific tasks and responsibilities to each team member. 5 28/05/2026 System Architecture Design: Analyze requirements and design the overall system architecture diagram for the TCG game hosted on AWS, ensuring scalability. 6 29/05/2026 Finalize UI/UX Design: Consolidate team feedback, refine, and finalize the basic User Interface and User Experience (UI/UX) design plan for the project. Achievements: Agreed on ideas, functional requirements, and task assignments for the internship project. Successfully built the initial test UI interface and prepared all artwork resources. Completed the system architecture diagram in preparation for the development process.

title: Cleanup Resources
content:
Overview After completing the workshop, you should remove all AWS resources created during the deployment to avoid unnecessary charges.
The resources used in this workshop include:
Amazon Cognito AWS Lambda Amazon API Gateway Amazon SQS Amazon DynamoDB AWS IAM (Users, Roles, and Policies) AWS Secrets Manager AWS Key Management Service (KMS) AWS Amplify Hosting Note: Only delete the resources created specifically for this workshop. Do not remove resources used by other projects.
Step 1. Delete AWS Lambda Functions Open the AWS Lambda Console. Select all Lambda functions created for the project. Choose Actions → Delete. Type confirm to proceed. After deletion, all Lambda functions will be permanently removed.
Step 2. Delete HTTP API and WebSocket API Open the Amazon API Gateway Console. Select the APIs created for this workshop. Choose Delete. Confirm the deletion. This removes all API endpoints, routes, and deployment stages.
Step 3. Delete Amazon SQS Queue Open the Amazon SQS Console. Select the project queue. Choose Delete. Confirm by entering the queue name. After deletion, the queue will no longer store or process messages.
Step 4. Delete DynamoDB Tables Open the Amazon DynamoDB Console. Select the project tables. Choose Delete Table. Enter the table name to confirm. All data stored in the table will be permanently removed.
Step 5. Delete Amazon Cognito User Pool Open the Amazon Cognito Console. Navigate to User Pools. Select the User Pool created for this workshop. Choose Delete User Pool. Confirm the deletion. All users and authentication settings associated with the User Pool will be permanently removed.
Step 6. Delete IAM Resources Finally, remove the IAM resources created for this workshop.
These include:
IAM User IAM Role Follow these steps:
Detach all policies from the IAM User or Role. Delete the IAM Role. Delete the IAM User. IAM Users and Roles cannot be deleted while they still have attached policies or active access keys.
Step 7. Delete AWS Secrets Manager Secrets If you used AWS Secrets Manager to store sensitive information such as Cognito Client Secrets or API keys, you should delete these secrets after completing the workshop.
Open the AWS Secrets Manager console. Select the secrets created for this project. Choose Delete secret. Confirm the deletion. Note: Only delete the secrets that were created specifically for this workshop.
Step 8. Delete AWS KMS Keys If your project uses AWS Key Management Service (KMS) to encrypt data or manage secrets, you should remove the Customer Managed Keys created during the workshop.
Open the AWS KMS console. Select Customer managed keys. Choose the key created for this project. Select Schedule key deletion. Choose a waiting period (for example, 7 days). Confirm the deletion request. AWS KMS does not allow immediate deletion. Keys must first be scheduled for deletion after the selected waiting period.
Step 9. Delete the AWS Amplify Application After completing the workshop, you can remove the application deployed with AWS Amplify Hosting.
Open the AWS Amplify Console. Select the deployed application. Navigate to App settings → General settings. Scroll to the bottom of the page. Choose Delete app. Enter the application name to confirm. After deletion:
The hosted website will become unavailable. AWS Amplify Hosting will stop serving the application. The deployment history will be permanently removed. Completion Congratulations!
You have successfully completed the workshop and cleaned up all AWS resources created throughout the project, including:
Amazon Cognito AWS Lambda Amazon API Gateway Amazon SQS Amazon DynamoDB AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) AWS Secrets Manager AWS Key Management Service (KMS) AWS Amplify Hosting Cleaning up these resources after completing the workshop helps you:
Avoid unnecessary AWS charges. Keep your AWS account organized and clean. Remove unused cloud resources. Prepare a clean environment for future workshops or projects.

title: Sharing and Feedback
content:
General Evaluation of the Program 1. Impressions of the program and learning environment
The First Cloud AI Journey (FCAJ) program has successfully cultivated an open, dynamic tech community atmosphere rather than a rigid office environment. This is an ideal setting for students to gain early exposure to AWS cloud infrastructure and master the process of migrating an application from a local environment to a live cloud deployment. The workspace at the AWS Vietnam office is highly professional, providing a comfortable atmosphere that allows interns to maximize their creative potential.
2. Support from Mentors and the Organizing Committee (Admin Team)
The mentor team is incredibly dedicated, constantly ready to provide technical support and project architecture guidance even outside working hours. I highly appreciate their oriented mentoring approach: empowering interns to independently research and experiment with solutions before offering suggestions, which greatly enhances proactive problem-solving skills. Additionally, the Admin Team has been extremely supportive in providing fully equipped AWS accounts, facilitating a smooth practical learning experience.
3. Alignment with academic major
The serverless game development project perfectly aligns with my Software Engineering major. It not only solidified my programming fundamentals but also expanded my knowledge in advanced domains not deeply covered in the university curriculum, such as NoSQL databases (Amazon DynamoDB), real-time communication (API Gateway WebSockets), and cloud cost optimization strategies.
4. Overall satisfaction with the program
I am absolutely satisfied with the program’s structured internship roadmap—from the fundamental hands-on Labs in the initial weeks to the autonomous research and full-scale development phase of the Chrono Genesis TCG project in the latter half.
Additional Feedback What are you most satisfied with during the internship?
The opportunity to directly build and deploy a complete Web application on the AWS cloud using real-world Serverless services. Furthermore, participating in high-quality tech events like the FCAJ Community Day allowed me to expand my professional network with industry experts.
What aspects of the program need improvement for future internship cohorts?
The registration and allocation process for in-person office working sessions could be better optimized for fairness. Currently, the randomized selection mechanism sometimes leads to unequal office opportunities among members. A more balanced scheduling approach would ensure everyone has equal chances for in-person collaboration.
Would you recommend this internship program to your friends?
Absolutely. This is a highly practical internship program that provides top-tier learning resources and serves as the best current environment for students to comprehensively develop their Cloud computing skills in general, and within the Amazon Web Services ecosystem in particular.
Proposals & Future Aspirations Experience Proposal: I hope the organizing team considers refining the approval process for attending training and events at the FCAJ office to provide more interns with practical exposure. Future Aspirations: I genuinely look forward to the opportunity to continue accompanying and contributing to the program in the future, whether as a mentor supporting upcoming cohorts or by participating in new strategic projects within the FCAJ community.

title: Week 7 Worklog
content: Week 7 Objectives: Configuring AWS Cognito, writing Serverless backend (Lambda, API Gateway) and creating DynamoDB database. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Reference Material 2 01/06/2026 Configure AWS Cognito & Login UI: Set up AWS Cognito service and integrate it into the project, alongside designing user registration and login interfaces. 3 02/06/2026 Integrate Social Login: Configure single sign-on functionality using social media accounts like Google and Facebook on AWS Cognito. 4 03/06/2026 Deploy Serverless Backend: Build a serverless backend architecture utilizing AWS Lambda for logic processing and Amazon API Gateway for API endpoint management. 5 04/06/2026 Set up DynamoDB database: Initialize and configure Amazon DynamoDB NoSQL database to securely store user information and game data. 6 05/06/2026 Build & Deploy System: Finalize server-side processing functions, then proceed to build and deploy the entire system to the AWS environment. Achievements: Successfully set up a secure user authentication system via AWS Cognito (including Social Login). Fully deployed the Serverless backend system with API Gateway and Lambda. Successfully initialized and connected the DynamoDB database.

title: Week 8 Worklog
content:
Week 8 Objectives: Developing realtime WebSocket, building matchmaking system and game ruling. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Progress 2 08/06/2026 Configure socket and build connection functionality between 2 players via socket. Completed
3 09/06/2026 Develop room creation and random matchmaking features for PvP mode. Completed
4 10/06/2026 Design and implement logic processing functions during gameplay covering possible in-game actions. Completed
5 11/06/2026 Write matchmaking logic based on elo, calculating elo points according to standard Swiss format ruling. Completed
6 12/06/2026 Test and fix bugs related to logic, ruling, and basic mechanics of the game. Completed
Achievements: WebSocket system is stable, allowing real-time connection between two players. Completed the random matchmaking system and Elo point calculation after each match. Core game logic functions (ruling) have been successfully deployed and tested.

title: Workshop Resources
content:
Below are the key resources used and created during the research and development of the project.
Project Source Code Visit the GitHub Repository to view the complete source code for the Chrono Genesis TCG serverless game project.
Product Demo Videos Please use headphones or turn on audio for the best experience!
General overview and login/registration of TCG WEB GAME Video introducing the overall interface, features & login/registration of CHRONO GENESIS TCG.
Core gameplay mechanics and TRIAL feature Video introducing the core gameplay mechanics of CHRONO GENESIS TCG.
AFK/Game freeze handling mechanism Video introducing the AFK/Game freeze handling mechanism of CHRONO GENESIS TCG.
Full gameplay demo Phuc Khang vs Anh Tien Full gameplay demo video of CHRONO GENESIS TCG.

title: Week 9 Worklog
content: Week 9 Objectives: Upgrading game UI design, integrating combat audio. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Reference Material 2 15/06/2026 UI Optimization: Adjust colors, refresh button designs, borders, and optimize CSS across the homepage, lobby, and game room interfaces. 3 16/06/2026 Player Info Board Design: Design and program the detailed player information display area (name, Elo ranking, avatar, win/loss ratio) within the PvP match room. 4 17/06/2026 Audio & Music Integration: Integrate background music into the lobby and match rooms, and set up specific sound effects corresponding to in-game actions. 5 18/06/2026 Audio State Management: Build a volume control slider interface and program a flexible toggle system (on/off) for managing background music and sound effects. 6 19/06/2026 Audio Testing & Refinement: Review and thoroughly fix any bugs related to the audio system, ensuring a smooth and uninterrupted listening experience. Achievements: Game UI is upgraded to be visually appealing, modern, and user-friendly. Audio system (background music, effects) works smoothly, with an intuitive volume control slider. Player information display area is arranged logically and accurately displays data from the backend.

title: Week 10 Worklog
content: Week 10 Objectives: Deploying the website to Amazon S3 & CloudFront and upgrading the UI. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Reference Material 2 22/06/2026 Static Web Deploy to S3 & CloudFront: Deploy the web application build to an Amazon S3 Bucket and configure the CloudFront Content Delivery Network (CDN) to optimize page load speeds. 3 23/06/2026 Domain & HTTPS Configuration: Set up a custom domain name and integrate SSL/HTTPS security certificates via AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to ensure data safety. 4 24/06/2026 UI Finalization & Bug Fixing: Conduct a comprehensive review, finalize the new interface design, and resolve any remaining UI display issues (bugs). 5 25/06/2026 Assets Optimization: Compress and optimize the size of images, icons, and other static resources to maximize website performance. 6 26/06/2026 CI/CD Pipeline Testing: Test and evaluate the entire Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) workflow, ensuring automated deployment processes run smoothly for future updates. Achievements: Web application is distributed globally at high speed thanks to S3 and CloudFront. Completely resolved UI bugs, providing a smooth experience for players. Page load performance significantly improved through asset optimization.

title: Week 11 Worklog
content: Week 11 Objectives: Improving UI/UX, adding QoL features, reinforcing ruling, and preparing for Workshop. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Reference Material 2 29/06/2026 Comprehensive System Testing: Execute end-to-end testing across all features including login, matchmaking, Elo calculation, and the audio system; simultaneously resolving any arising bugs. 3 30/06/2026 Quality of Life (QoL) Enhancements: Develop and integrate additional Quality of Life features based on feedback gathered during the testing phase. 4 01/07/2026 Ruling Reinforcement & Security: Review and reinforce game rule logic to thoroughly handle edge cases, and implement measures to prevent potential cheating. 5 02/07/2026 Workshop Material Preparation: Outline content, draft detailed documentation, and build a structured presentation script in preparation for the final project Workshop. 6 03/07/2026 Internal Demo (Final Review): Organize an internal team demo session to evaluate overall project progress, identifying and addressing final adjustments before the official presentation. Achievements: The TCG game system runs smoothly without any critical bugs hindering the experience. Game rules and logic are firmly reinforced. Workshop content is fully prepared and ready for the final project presentation.

title: Week 12 Worklog
content: Week 12 Objectives: Develop and test the game. Tasks to be carried out this week: Day Date Task Reference Material 2 - 6 06/07 - 10/07/2026 Development & Testing: Focus on finalizing core game features and conduct comprehensive testing to ensure system quality. Achievements: Successfully completed the development and testing of the entire game. The system operates stably without critical bugs and is ready for final review.
